entry.title: rhinoplasty - new results
entry.content:

… undergoing cleft rhinoplasty using a combination of Dibbell’s and Tajima’s
techniques within an open structural rhinoplasty approach, … This study included 34
primary patients with unilateral cleft nasal deformity (mean age: 26.6 years) who …

[HTML] Two Decades of Male Cosmetic Surgery: A Single-center Observational Study in Lebanon

M Aoude, K Sleilati, G Prince, E Kassouf, G Gharghour… - Plastic and Reconstructive …, 2026
… The decline in rhinoplasty volumes across both sexes likely reflects the
decentralization of rhinoplasty expertise in Lebanon, with more private … providing
these services has redistributed patient flow, leading to a relative decline in …

[HTML] Advancing Nasal Contouring With Superficial Musculoaponeurotic System Flap–Wrapped Diced Cartilage: A Technical Innovation

K Sami, OM Hassan - Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery–Global Open, 2026
… The concept diverges from earlier uses of SMAS in rhinoplasty, which have
primarily involved camouflage or resurfacing roles, and from SMAS-based periosteal
flaps that serve structural purposes. In contrast, the described construct is designed …

Lesion Location and Surgical Outcomes in Pediatric Frontonasal and Orbital Dermoid Cysts: A Systematic Review

A Gierlotka, I Bielecki - International Journal of Pediatric Otorhinolaryngology, 2026
Objective This systematic review aimed to answer the clinically relevant question of
whether lesion location (midline versus periorbital) is associated with differences in
risk of intracranial extension, operative strategy, and recurrence in pediatric …

A Kruayatidee, P Shakoori, JA Hernandez, JW Calvert - Journal of Craniofacial Surgery
… timing of flap division will allow surgeons to achieve optimal functional and
aesthetic outcomes in reconstructing major rhinoplasty defects. … Achieving an
ideal tip esthetic is arguably the most complex part of rhinoplasty. Jack Anderson …

[PDF] Wrinkles in Time: Uncovering the Early History of Cosmetic Plastic Surgery (1850-1890)

Y Zhang, DJ Soares - Aesthetic Surgery Journal Open Forum, 2026
… This shift to include cosmetic indications is 9 particularly evident in the evolution
of rhinoplasty between 1840 and 1887, during which an 10 increasing emphasis
was placed not merely on replacing an amputated nose but on enhancing the 11 …

Korea during the Japanese colonial period, the aesthetic ideals that would later

and aesthetic surgery departments [14,15,16]. Consequently, in public hospitals,

than aesthetic surgery, these findings provide a physiologic rationale for

characterized by involuntary eyelid contractions that can disrupt tear film stability
and corneal morphology. Botulinum toxin type A (BTX-A) injection is the standard …
